text: No IV access: • Midazolam buccal 0.5mg/kg (max 15mg) or • Diazepam PR 0.5 mg/kg Status Epilepticus Flowchart ABC High ﬂow oxygen Check and correct BGL Get IV access IV access: • Midazolam IV/IO 0.15mg/kg or • Diazepam IV/IO 0.25 mg/kg Wait 5 minutes If still ﬁtting Get IV or IO access Alternative treatment if no IV/IO access or failure of 2nd line management Paraldehyde PR 0.4mls/kg (max 5mls) dilute 1:1 in olive oil or 0.9% saline Repeat/give: • Midazolam buccal 0.5mg/kg (max 15mg) or • Diazepam PR 0.5 mg/kg Repeat/give: • Midazolam IV/IO 0.15mg/kg or • Diazepam IV/IO 0.25 mg/kg Wait 5 minutes If still ﬁtting Get IV access or consider IO IV/IO access No IV/IO access Give second line anti-epileptic agent: • Phenytoin IV/IO 20 mg/kg in 0.9% saline over 30 mins (max 1g) ECG monitoring + observations every 5 minutes or • Phenobartitone IV/IO 20mg/kg in 0.9% saline over 15 minutes (drug of choice in Neonates) or • Levetiracetam IV 40mg/kg in 0.9% saline over 5 minutes (max 3g) Single dose only If still ﬁtting after loading second line agent: • Intubate with rapid sequence induction • Seek assistance from most experienced available doctor (PICU, Anaesthetist) • Admit to PICU • Thiopentone 4mg/kg IV/IO • Atropine 0.02mg/kg IV/IO • Suxamethonium 1 - 2mg/kg IV Rapid Sequence Induction We take no responsibility for the use of these guidelines outside our institution. Please read our disclaimer.
